fyPerformance: FY26 revenue grew 29.2% in USD terms, marking one of the strongest growth performances in the sector.
Q4 delivered 2% sequential constant-currency growth and 21.2% YoY growth in USD terms.
Q4 EBIT margin stood at 16.6%, while free cash flow to PAT reached 110% for the quarter.
Healthcare and high-tech grew 98%, travel grew 62%, BFS grew 12%, and emerging verticals grew 27%.
Top 10 accounts grew 40.4% YoY and contributed 30.8% of revenue, reflecting broad account mining.

guidance.value: Management expects exceptional performance to continue
Growth story seen as still early
Sustained guidance around 70% FCF to PAT

highlights: Coforge closed 21 large deals in FY26, strengthening visibility for the next growth phase.
Top 5 accounts grew 45.8% YoY and accounts 6-10 grew 30%, showing strong client expansion.
The company highlighted a long-term track record of 21.7% revenue CAGR and 24.6% EBIT CAGR.
Growth remained diversified across healthcare, travel, BFS, government and emerging verticals.
Free cash flow remained strong, with Q4 FCF to PAT materially above the steady-state target.
Management commentary stayed confident on FY27 execution and continued compounding.
Coforge Ltd.quote: "The growth story is not even half done."

fyPerformance: FY26 revenue stood at 3,300 Cr, growing 19% YoY, with USD revenue of 374 Mn.
Q4 revenue was 886 Cr, up 17% YoY and 4% QoQ, supported by steady client demand.
Healthcare and life sciences grew 66%, financial services grew 32%, and CPG and retail grew 12%.
Q4 adjusted EBITDA grew 28% YoY with margin expanding to 22%.
Net income grew 30% to 287 Cr, while operating cash flow stood at 409 Cr with 70% conversion.

guidance.value: Net revenue retention at 117% for FY26
Annualized recurring revenue up about 77% YoY
Long-term debt repaid after year end

highlights: Fractal delivered broad-based growth across healthcare, financial services and CPG/retail.
Europe grew 34% and the Americas grew 20%, offsetting softer APAC performance.
Full-year adjusted EBITDA margin stood at 17.6% even after meaningful R&D investment.
Q4 net income more than doubled YoY to 116 Cr, helped by scale and operating leverage.
NPS remained very high at 81, supporting strong renewal and expansion behavior.
The balance sheet strengthened with 2,052 Cr cash, including IPO proceeds.
Fractal Analytics Ltd.quote: "Net revenue retention remained strong at 117% for the full year."

fyPerformance: FY26 revenue crossed 2,315 Cr, growing 12.3% in rupee terms and 9.2% in constant currency.
Operating margin for FY26 stood at 17.4%, broadly stable versus 17.3% in the previous year.
FY26 PAT stood at 213 Cr, growing 15%, while adjusted PAT was 279 Cr.
The company ended the year with 306 active customers and a healthier deal pipeline.
Q4 operating margin stood at 17.5%, with operating profit of 106 Cr.

guidance.value: 12.5% guidance, aspirational 15%
18-18.5% operating margin target
Targeting 1,000 AI and GenAI focused people by FY27 end

highlights: Management reconfirmed FY27 revenue growth guidance of 12.5%, with an aspirational 15% target.
The Q4 pipeline grew 27%, improving revenue visibility for the next year.
The company plans a meaningful hiring and capability ramp in FY27.
AI and GenAI are becoming a core investment area, with a 1,000-member focused team planned.
Margins are expected to improve to 18-18.5% in FY27 as operating leverage builds.
Attrition remained an area to watch at around 17%.
Happiest Minds Technologies Ltd.quote: "The Board reconfirmed our FY27 growth guidance of 12.5%."

fyPerformance: FY26 revenue stood at 3,510.5 Cr, growing 23.6% in INR terms and 18.2% in USD terms.
Q4 became the first quarter with revenue above 1,000 Cr, up 6.5% QoQ and 32.8% YoY.
FY26 adjusted EBITDA grew 20.8% to 679.3 Cr, while Q4 adjusted EBITDA was 188.9 Cr.
Adjusted PAT grew 12.7% to 458.3 Cr, while reported PAT stood at 401.1 Cr.
Operating cash flow was strong at 650.8 Cr, equivalent to 162% of PAT.

guidance.value: $10 Mn+ omnichannel deal to contribute from Q1 FY27
Customers above $1 Mn grew from 41 to 53
BioPharm, Warn and Cake Communications added in FY26

highlights: Top 20 customer revenue grew from 2,208.2 Cr to 2,520 Cr, showing strong account expansion.
Active customers increased from 73 to 91 during the year.
Customers contributing over $1 Mn grew to 53, strengthening the quality of revenue.
Tectonic secured 5 customers, with 2 pilots moving into long-term engagements.
Three acquisitions expanded capabilities across medical, regulatory and creative services.
Cash generation remained strong, with operating cash flow materially ahead of reported PAT.
Indegene Ltd.quote: "Q4 was our first ever quarter above 1,000 Cr revenue."

fyPerformance: FY26 total income stood at 3,161 Cr, growing 23% YoY.
License-linked revenue grew 34% to 1,667 Cr, supported by platform, license and AMC growth.
Platform revenue rose 141% to 580 Cr, reflecting stronger IP-led monetization.
FY26 EBITDA grew 16% to 703 Cr, while Non-GAAP EBITDA stood at 756 Cr.
PAT after exceptional items stood at 345 Cr, with cash and equivalents of 1,257 Cr and zero debt.

guidance.value: Crossed 12,000 Cr
99 active deals, 21 wins in FY26
Q4 ARR at 1,247 Cr

highlights: The company added 59 new customers and completed 91 digital transformations during FY26.
Q4 total income stood at 884 Cr, with platform revenue of 162 Cr and license revenue of 152 Cr.
Q4 EBITDA was 221 Cr and Q4 PBT stood at 162 Cr.
Collections were healthy at 791 Cr in Q4, supporting balance sheet strength.
The pipeline crossed 12,000 Cr, giving strong medium-term visibility.
The company filed close to 100 patents during the year, taking total filings to 125.
Intellect Design Arena Ltd.quote: "Our platform revenue grew 141% during the year."

fyPerformance: FY26 revenue stood at 3,193 Cr, growing nearly 20% YoY.
FY26 EBITDA grew 38% to 1,091 Cr, with EBITDA margin around 34%.
FY26 PAT grew 48% to 721 Cr, reflecting strong operating leverage.
Operating cash flow grew 99% to 863 Cr, while free cash flow stood around 721 Cr.
Q4 revenue was 857 Cr, up 18.5% YoY and 5.2% QoQ, with 13% constant-currency growth.

guidance.value: Around 35%
More than 600 clients
Agentic AI across documentation, coding and prior authorization

highlights: Q4 EBITDA was around 300 Cr, growing 33% YoY and 6.6% QoQ.
Top 10 customer revenue stood at 452 Cr in Q4.
Headcount was 13,331, up 5.3% YoY, while Q4 revenue grew despite QoQ headcount reduction.
The sales and marketing team expanded to 66 FTEs to support growth.
Agentic AI products such as Scribble Now, Scribble Select and MyCare Hub are gaining focus.
The TruBridge transaction is expected to add another strategic growth pillar.
Inventurus Knowledge Solutions Ltd.quote: "We delivered nearly 20% revenue growth with 38% EBITDA growth."

fyPerformance: FY26 net new TCV exceeded $2.1 Bn, the highest ever for the company.
FY26 revenue grew 6.7% in constant currency, with Q4 revenue at $463 Mn.
Q4 revenue grew 2.5% QoQ and 7.1% YoY in constant currency.
Direct revenue contributed 98.6% of total revenue and grew 9.2% YoY in Q4 constant currency.
FY26 EBIT margin stood at 15.3%, while Q4 EBIT margin improved to 15.4%.

guidance.value: 64% of Q4 wins were AI-led
69% of pipeline AI-led
Direct BFS grew 18.6% in FY26

highlights: Q4 net new TCV stood at $407 Mn, adding to strong FY26 deal momentum.
BFS remained the core vertical at 76.5% of revenue, with strong YoY growth in Q4.
Insurance grew 7.3% QoQ and 46.5% YoY, adding a high-growth vertical tailwind.
Operating profit grew 7.2% QoQ and 15% YoY in Q4.
The company highlighted strong AI-led demand through NeoIP and related platforms.
Client bucket expansion continued, including movement into larger revenue categories.
Mphasis Ltd.quote: "FY26 net new TCV crossed $2.1 billion, our highest ever."

fyPerformance: FY26 revenue stood at $1,654.4 Mn, growing 17.4% YoY.
Rupee revenue grew 23.5% to 14,748.5 Cr.
FY26 EBIT grew 31.5% to 2,303.5 Cr, with EBIT margin expanding 90 bps to 15.6%.
Q4 revenue was $436 Mn, up 3.2% QoQ and 16.2% YoY, marking the 24th sequential growth quarter.
Q4 PAT grew 20.4% QoQ and 33.7% YoY, with PAT margin at 13.1%.

guidance.value: $428.9 Mn TCV and $445.1 Mn ACV
Total FY26 dividend of 40 per share
24 consecutive quarters of revenue growth

highlights: Q4 rupee revenue grew 7.4% QoQ and 25.1% YoY to 4,055.9 Cr.
Q4 EBIT was 659.2 Cr, with margin improving 70 bps to 16.3%.
North America grew 17.2% and Europe grew 26.7% during FY26.
BFS grew 28.4%, high-tech and emerging industries grew 13.8%, and healthcare grew 10.1%.
Q4 bookings remained strong with both TCV and ACV above quarterly revenue.
The company maintained consistent growth with improving profitability.
Persistent Systems Ltd.quote: "Q4 marked our 24th sequential quarter of revenue growth."

fyPerformance: Q4 revenue stood at 2,024.3 Cr, growing 29.1% YoY.
Q4 adjusted EBITDA was 503.6 Cr, with margin at 24.9%.
FY26 adjusted EBITDA stood at 1,820 Cr, with margin of 25.3%, ahead of guidance.
FY26 adjusted PAT grew 39.5% YoY to 1,130.6 Cr, with margin expanding to 15.7%.
Three-year CAGR was 16.2% for revenue, 17.7% for adjusted EBITDA and 31.7% for adjusted PAT in dollar terms.

guidance.value: $30.7 Mn Q4 commercial ACV potential
Q4 operating cash flow conversion at 104.6%
Momentum expected to continue, though deal timing can be lumpy

highlights: Q4 commercial wins were spread across 18 existing clients and 2 new clients.
Employee base stood at 46,860 at the end of Q4.
Cash position was 903.8 Cr at the end of Q4, supported by strong collections.
Adjusted PAT growth remained ahead of revenue growth due to operating leverage.
Management highlighted healthy momentum into FY27 despite normal timing variability in deals.
The company continued to scale in healthcare operations with strong profitability.
Sagility Ltd.quote: "Adjusted EBITDA margin came in ahead of guidance at 25.3%."

fyPerformance: Q4 delivered more than 10% sequential revenue growth, with reported revenue up 15.1% QoQ.
Services revenue grew 12% QoQ in constant currency, led by automotive and aerospace demand.
Automotive grew 13.6% QoQ in USD terms, while Aerospace and IHM grew 4.6% QoQ.
Technology Solutions grew 12% sequentially, with education up 36% sequentially.
Q4 EBITDA grew 30.7% QoQ to 252 Cr, with margin improving 200 bps to 16%.

guidance.value: Double-digit organic topline growth excluding ES-Tec
Operating margin run rate above 18%
4 large Q4 deals plus 2 milestone wins after quarter end

highlights: The company won large programs across North American commercial vehicles, European autos and Tier-1 suppliers.
April wins included a European luxury OEM PLM transformation and a Japanese OEM full-vehicle program.
Embedded Software has compounded at 60% CAGR over the last three years.
Aerospace revenue has grown 8x over four years and crossed a $40 Mn annual run rate.
Management is targeting double-digit organic growth in FY27, excluding ES-Tec.
Operating margin exit run rate is expected to move above 18% by the end of FY27.
Tata Technologies Ltd.quote: "We expect to exit FY27 at an operating margin run rate of over 18%."
